turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi, I’ve just realised I lost my wallet. I’m at Hobart Airport, near the check‑in area. I’d like to check with the lost‑and‑found desk.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I’m sorry to hear that. To help the search, could you describe the wallet and where you last saw it?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: It’s a black wallet, about standard size.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thanks. When exactly did you last see it, and where were you standing or walking?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I last saw it at around  p.m. I was near check‑in counter 2, then I walked toward Gate 3. I think I might have dropped it near the seating area where close to the coffee shop.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it. Do you remember if the wallet was zipped shut or left open?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I usually keep it zipped. It had quite a few cards inside and some cash.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: About how much cash roughly, and which cards were most important? That way, if someone finds it, they’ll know which items to highlight.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: There was around AUS$80 in cash, and important cards include my driver’s licence and a local gym membership card.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good details. Do you need to fill out a lost‑item form now?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, please. Could you also tell me what happens after I submit it?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: We’ll log your details and the item. If someone brings in a matching wallet, we’ll call or email you using the contact information you provide. We’ll also keep your form for the retention period and check it regularly.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: How long do you keep unclaimed wallets?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Typically, we keep unclaimed wallets for 30 days first. If it’s not claimed, we may transfer it to the airport police or follow local rules.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Could I leave my contact details now so you can call me if it’s found?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, please. I’ll need your full name, mobile number, and email address. May I also ask for a backup contact in case we can’t reach you on the number provided?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: My name is Charissa, my phone +61xxx xxx xxx, email xxxxxx@email.com.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thank you. Noted — Charissa. Are you currently at the airport, or will you be leaving soon? That helps us decide if we should also check with desks near your location right away.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I’m still here in the check‑in area, but I’ll head to the gate soon.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Understood. Since you’re still on site, would you like us to inform the staff at the information desk and the coffee shop near your last known location? They can keep an eye out and alert us immediately if someone hands in a matching wallet.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, please do that. Thank you so much for your help.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re welcome. If you have more details later, just call this desk or come back in person. Have a safe trip.
